arguments.contentBrief: An introduction to OpenDesign, an internal design-system platform: one design system compiled into five composable surfaces (slide decks, dashboards, project pages, personal pages, technical explainers), served from a gallery of 65 live worlds. World-templates carry all the craft (layout, color, motion); typed fills carry all the content; MCP compose tools deterministically select a template and hand back a fill skeleton, and the open-design skill orchestrates three routes - COMPOSE (source content to finished experience), BORROW (lift one part's treatment across worlds via public data-part-id anchors), and AUDIT (severity-ranked design-quality punch list). Quality is enforced by contracts: a certifier, part-id tests, a validate_fill loop with render budgets, and a verification rig with screenshot judging. The deck explains the division of labor, the three routes, the gallery estate, and the quality loop so engineers and design-minded PMs can adopt the system.

error.message: pinTemplateId "deck-innovation-showcase" is not a live slide-deck template.
details: Live slide-deck templates: cutover (deck-cloud-migration), dgm-blueprint (deck-dgm-blueprint), dgm-circuit (deck-dgm-circuit), dgm-gazette (deck-dgm-gazette), dgm-isometric (deck-dgm-isometric), dgm-sketchnote (deck-dgm-sketchnote), quarter (deck-quarterly-business-review), tminus (deck-product-launch).
remediation: Pin one of this surface's template ids/experienceIds (typically from a previous call's alternatives), or drop pinTemplateId to let scoring decide.

disposition: BLOCKED - the fill target named for the spatial-canvas grammar specimen (deck-innovation-showcase) is a hand-built world, not a composable world-template. No fill was authored; see notes.md.
